items.hook: Legend makes Abdias first bishop of Babylon and one of the Seventy Apostles who are collectively mentioned in the Gospel of Luke 10:1–20.
items.body: Saints Simon and Jude allegedly consecrated him as the first Bishop of Babylon. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: What the Karlovy Vary, Galway and Munich film festivals do best - The Screen Podcast
A voyage of discovery: an idiot’s guide to reading The Odyssey
items.body:
Christopher Nolan’s film adaptation of the ancient Greek epic has sparked a new appetite for an old classic. Here are the translations, podcasts and audiobooks that make the Homeric world more approachable

The Odyssey was once all

items.hook: Celebrated by the Carmelite Order–but doubted by modern historians–as the day when Saint Simon Stock had a vision of the Virgin Mary.
items.body: The Order of the Brothers of the Blessed Virgin Mary of Mount Carmel, known as the Carmelites or sometimes by synecdoche known simply as Carmel, is a mendicant order in the Catholic Church for both men and women. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: "Working Day and Night" is a song by the American recording artist Michael Jackson.
items.body: It is the third track from his fifth studio album, Off the Wall (1979). Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
England's defeat to Argentina in the World Cup semi-final attracts an audience of 24 million on the BBC.

items.hook: William Fitzhugh was an American planter, legislator and patriot during the American Revolutionary War who served as a delegate to the Continental Congress for Virginia in 1779, as well as many terms in the House of Burgesses and both houses of the Virginia General Assembly following the Commonwealth's formation.
items.body: His Stafford County home, Chatham Manor, is on the National Register for Historic Places and serves as the National Park Service Headquarters for the Fredericksburg and Spotsylvania National Military Park. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Three Roman legates place - acting illegally on their own authority - a bull of excommunication against Michael I Cerularius, the patriarch of Constantinople, and his followers on the altar of Hagia Sophia during Saturday afternoon divine liturgy. Historians frequently describe the event as the formal start of the East–West Schism.
items.body: A papal legate or apostolic legate is a personal representative of the Pope to foreign nations, to some other part of the Catholic Church, or to representatives of a state or monarchy. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Chung Soon-yeong is a mathematics professor in the Department of Mathematics and Convergence Biomedical Engineering at Sogang University.
items.body: He was the fifth president of the National Institute for Mathematical Sciences and a vice president of the Korean Mathematical Society. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Antonio María Claret y Clará founds the Congregation of the Missionary Sons of the Immaculate Heart of Mary, popularly known as the Claretians in Vic, in the province of Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.
items.body: Anthony Mary Claret, was a Spanish Catholic prelate and missionary who served as Archbishop of Santiago de Cuba and was the confessor of Queen Isabella II. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Rickie, Melvin and Dean McCullough to leave Radio 1 in schedule shake-up
items.body: Jeremiah Asiamah will join Charlie Hedges in the Live Lounge, among other changes.

items.hook: The Spanish town of Arjona declares independence and names its native Muhammad ibn Yusuf as ruler. This marks the Muhammad's first rise to prominence; he later established the Nasrid Emirate of Granada, the last independent Muslim state in Spain.
items.body: Arjona is a municipality in the province of Jaén, Andalusia, Spain. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Emperor Haile Selassie signs the first constitution of Ethiopia.
items.body: The emperor of Ethiopia, also known as the Atse, was the hereditary ruler of the Ethiopian Empire, from at least the 13th century until the abolition of the monarchy in 1975. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Iraqi President Ahmed Hassan al-Bakr resigns and is replaced by Saddam Hussein.
items.body: The president of the Republic of Iraq is the head of state of Iraq. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: In 2017, this ground-based telescope was renamed the Goode Solar Telescope (GST).
items.body: Goode is an American theoretical physicist also working in observational astronomy and its instrumentation. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Sri Lanka Institute of Advanced Technological Education is a statutory body in Sri Lanka coming under the purview of the Higher Education Ministry and offering Higher National Diploma courses.
items.body: At present, it manages and supervises eighteen provincial Advanced Technological Institutes throughout the island. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Odyssey is a 'colossal piece of cinema', critics say in rave reviews
items.body: The film, starring Matt Damon and Zendaya, is director Sir Christopher Nolan's first since Oppenheimer.

items.hook: Claudia Winkleman quits chat show after one series
items.body: Winkleman said she was "incredibly grateful" for the opportunity, but had been "too nervous" to enjoy it.

items.hook: Worry Doll by Laura McPhee-Browne review – a sensual, sinister novel about the horrors of desire
items.body:
Two women meet on a train and tumble into an all-consuming affair told from both sides – who have very different stories
